design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: New flagship topic: proximal difference-in-differences bridge frontier with negative controls. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Miao-Geng-Tchetgen proximal causal inference, Tchetgen-Tchetgen negative-control bridge work, standard two-period and staggered DiD identification, and recent papers on difference-in-differences with negative controls. Our theorem is not those because the focal object is a finite pretrend-proxy bridge operator that sharply separates three regimes: ordinary parallel-trends DiD identifies ATT, proximal negative-control DiD identifies ATT even when parallel trends fails, and neither identifies ATT. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an explicit 2-group x 3-period x binary-proxy witness with observed negative-control outcome and exposure, a bridge-rank condition computed from pre-period moments, and a non-equivalence theorem showing the proximal bridge and parallel-trends restrictions are neither nested nor equivalent. If the proposal reduces to generic proximal completeness, ordinary DiD placebo tests, support coverage, or a definition-unfold rank condition without a worked witness and non-equivalence class, pivot or stop early.

proposed_from.topic: New flagship topic: proximal difference-in-differences bridge frontier with negative controls. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Miao-Geng-Tchetgen proximal causal inference, Tchetgen-Tchetgen negative-control bridge work, standard two-period and staggered DiD identification, and recent papers on difference-in-differences with negative controls. Our theorem is not those because the focal object is a finite pretrend-proxy bridge operator that sharply separates three regimes: ordinary parallel-trends DiD identifies ATT, proximal negative-control DiD identifies ATT even when parallel trends fails, and neither identifies ATT. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an explicit 2-group x 3-period x binary-proxy witness with observed negative-control outcome and exposure, a bridge-rank condition computed from pre-period moments, and a non-equivalence theorem showing the proximal bridge and parallel-trends restrictions are neither nested nor equivalent. If the proposal reduces to generic proximal completeness, ordinary DiD placebo tests, support coverage, or a definition-unfold rank condition without a worked witness and non-equivalence class, pivot or stop early.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art is the failed eid_proximal_did_bridge_frontier_v1 and v2 v1 draft. Those attempted moment-row witnesses or a singular bridge interval; this kernel changes the focal object to Sigma(P), a finite regime signature with a raw-cell bridge-only witness and a non-nesting conjecture.","published_axis":"Closest published papers give proxy bridge identification, negative-control DiD calibration, generalized DiD, or pretrend sensitivity. None provides a common-support finite signature proving ordinary parallel trends and double-proxy bridge identification are neither nested nor equivalent."}
seed_list: Construct the calibration-residual wedge W_cal(P) and conjecture it is a strict finite comparator between scalar NC-DiD calibration and proximal bridge DiD.
Give a full rational-valued 2-group x 3-period x binary-proxy law catalogue certifying PT-only, proximal-only, and neither-identifies regimes.
Prove finite non-nesting of ordinary parallel trends, generalized DiD equi-confounding, and proximal negative-control bridge restrictions on common support.
Separate bridge-rank diagnostics from pretrend, placebo, and lead-coefficient diagnostics by an open finite class with disagreeing decisions.
Define a bridge-rank failure interval for singular B_P and conjecture sharp ATT bounds when scalar calibration is admissible but bridge uniqueness fails.
Construct a double-proxy exposure-indexed bridge estimator and conjecture an asymptotic linear expansion under fixed finite proxy support.
Prove scalar NC-DiD calibration is representable as a rank-one restriction of the exposure-indexed bridge equation and characterize its residual wedge.
Give a finite witness where generalized DiD equi-confounding identifies ATT but the proximal bridge equation is inconsistent.
Construct a common-support law where a Rambachan-Roth-style pretrend sensitivity band accepts while the proximal bridge-rank condition fails.
Promote the 2x3 binary witness to an open neighborhood theorem where determinant and residual signs classify the bridge/scalar/PT branches.

seed_details.one_line: Construct the calibration-residual wedge W_cal(P) and conjecture it is a strict finite comparator between scalar NC-DiD calibration and proximal bridge DiD.

anchor_paper.author: Dazheng Zhang, Bingyu Zhang, Huiyuan Wang, Yiwen Lu, Charles J. Wolock, Wenjie Hu, Linbo Wang, George Hripcsak, Yong Chen
anchor_paper.claim: NC-DiD calibrates DiD with negative-control outcomes but does not use an NCE-indexed bridge rank.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: uses negative control outcomes (NCOs) both before and after the intervention

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/frontier stronger because W_cal is a computed observable wedge with scalar residual and bridge correction, not a placebo test.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from MiaoShiLiTchetgen2024 because bridge equations already identify causal effects with NCO and NCE.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed compares a scalar NC-DiD calibration map to a finite exposure-indexed bridge map and computes strict disagreement from panel preperiod cells.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ZhangEtAl2025NCDID because their NCO calibration has no binary NCE-indexed bridge matrix, determinant, or scalar residual wedge.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the bridge equation is not the contribution; the new object is the comparator wedge W_cal(P) with a worked non-equivalence law.

adversarial_referee.instance: The wedge could be only a named diagnostic, not a theorem-level focal object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: Section 9 computes W_cal, theta_prox, theta_did, theta_ATT, and scalar residual from raw cells.

adversarial_referee.instance: Zhang et al. may not claim proximal bridge equivalence.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the comparison is to scalar calibration practice, not to a claimed equivalence.

adversarial_referee.instance: A single binary table might look hand tuned.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the conjecture promotes the witness to an open finite class via determinant and residual inequalities.

seed_details.one_line: Give a full rational-valued 2-group x 3-period x binary-proxy law catalogue certifying PT-only, proximal-only, and neither-identifies regimes.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: The prior bridge-frontier run lacked full finite observed/latent laws.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: moment rows rather than a full finite observed/latent law computing every object from Section 6 primitives

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Witness stronger because every branch is computed from common-support raw cells, not asserted from moment rows.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just repairing a failed internal proposal, not a literature-level theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: The repair targets the published NC-DiD/proximal gap and supplies the finite object missing from both the papers and the banked run.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not the banked v1 proposal because v1 named regimes without full primitive laws or latent completion.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the finite catalogue is a new witness certificate, not an unfold of bridge nonemptiness.

adversarial_referee.instance: Three finite laws could be corner cases.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: all cells are positive and the main conjecture extends by open determinant/residual inequalities.

adversarial_referee.instance: Internal reviewer signal might not be enough as flagship anchor.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the seed is cross-linked to Miao 2018, Miao-Shi 2024, and Zhang 2025.

adversarial_referee.instance: A witness alone may not be a flagship theorem.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: witness supports the comparator wedge conjecture rather than standing alone.

seed_details.one_line: Prove finite non-nesting of ordinary parallel trends, generalized DiD equi-confounding, and proximal negative-control bridge restrictions on common support.

anchor_paper.author: Tamar Sofer, David B. Richardson, Elena Colicino, Joel Schwartz, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: DiD can be interpreted as negative-outcome control but uses scale-sensitive bias-equivalence restrictions.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: we interpret the difference-in-differences as a negative outcome control (NOC) approach

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption comparison stronger because it asks for strict non-inclusion laws, not broader sufficient conditions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from differing assumptions in Sofer et al. and proximal papers.
seed_details.rebuttal: Non-nesting of restrictions requires common-support laws with ATT calculations; prose comparison does not prove strict separation.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not SoferRichardsonColicinoSchwartzTchetgen2016 because it adds an NCE bridge branch and computes strict non-inclusion laws.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: finite common-support countermodels create a non-equivalence class rather than a literature taxonomy.

adversarial_referee.instance: Non-nesting needs both directions and a failure branch.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: Section 9 includes PT-only, proximal-only, and neither branches; generalized DiD is reserved for extension.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could identify ATT, ATU, or ATE jointly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: locked target is ATT because DiD and NC-DiD comparators target treated-switcher effects.

adversarial_referee.instance: Universal DiD targets odds-ratio equi-confounding, not additive PT.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: proposal cites it as a separate branch, not as the same restriction.

seed_details.one_line: Separate bridge-rank diagnostics from pretrend, placebo, and lead-coefficient diagnostics by an open finite class with disagreeing decisions.

anchor_paper.author: Ashesh Rambachan and Jonathan Roth
anchor_paper.claim: Pretrend information supports sensitivity analysis for violations of parallel trends, not bridge-rank identification.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: sensitivity analyses showing what causal conclusions can be drawn under various restrictions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Diagnostic extension stronger because it proves disagreement between diagnostics on an open class, not another sensitivity band.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just another pretrend test.
seed_details.rebuttal: Bridge rank conditions on P(N|A,G) can vary while ordinary pretrend means and placebo contrasts remain fixed.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not RambachanRoth2023 because the object is an NCO/NCE bridge matrix, not a deviation set for parallel trends.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: nonrepresentation by scalar diagnostics is a new finite-class obstruction.

adversarial_referee.instance: No named estimator or rate accompanies the diagnostic.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: ExactID proposal is exempt from Panel estimator-duality; focal object is W_cal.

adversarial_referee.instance: May show bridge rank sufficient but not necessary.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the seed is nonrepresentation, not a sufficiency theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: Disagreement could be a one-point artifact.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: determinant and residual inequalities define an open finite neighborhood.

seed_details.one_line: Define a bridge-rank failure interval for singular B_P and conjecture sharp ATT bounds when scalar calibration is admissible but bridge uniqueness fails.

anchor_paper.claim: Proxy rank identifies causal effects, and failures of rank motivate nonidentification/testing.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: at least two independent proxy variables satisfying a certain rank condition

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption weakening weaker unless singular branch yields a computable interval; kept as lower-ranked backup.
seed_details.referee_objection: This collapses to generic rank-deficient proximal partial identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: It only survives if the interval is tied to DiD preperiod cells and scalar calibration status, not generic bridge null space.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not MiaoGengTchetgen2018 because the target is a DiD ATT interval indexed by preperiod bridge failure and scalar calibration.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless the interval has a closed-form non-LP support function.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a double-proxy exposure-indexed bridge estimator and conjecture an asymptotic linear expansion under fixed finite proxy support.

proposed_from.literature_map:
1. Miao-Geng-Tchetgen 2018 Biometrika proves proxy-rank identification under unmeasured confounding with two independent proxies; DOI https://doi.org/10.1093/biomet/asy038.
2. Miao-Shi-Li-Tchetgen 2024 STRF gives a double-negative-control outcome bridge but not a finite DiD bridge frontier; DOI https://doi.org/10.1080/24754269.2024.2390748.
3. Cui-Pu-Shi-Miao-Tchetgen 2024 JASA develops semiparametric proximal inference around bridge equations, not a finite branch catalogue.
4. Sofer-Richardson-Colicino-Schwartz-Tchetgen 2016 Statistical Science interprets DiD as negative outcome control and exposes scale-sensitive bias-equivalence restrictions.
5. Richardson-Ye-Tchetgen 2023 Epidemiology and Tchetgen-Park-Richardson 2024 Epidemiology generalize DiD via equi-confounding, not double-proxy bridge rank.
6. Zhang-Zhang-Wang et al. 2025 npj Digital Medicine is the closest NC-DiD calibration comparator; it uses NCOs before and after intervention but no NCE bridge operator; DOI https://doi.org/10.1038/s41746-025-01821-w.
7. Callaway-SantAnna 2021, Goodman-Bacon 2021, Sun-Abraham 2021, and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille 2020 define the modern parallel-trends/staggered-DiD comparison class.
8. Rambachan-Roth 2023 studies robust inference under deviations from parallel trends, not proxy bridge identification.
9. The banked v1 CausalSmith run named bridge-frontier regimes but was rejected for moment rows rather than a full finite law and latent completion.
10. Net gap: no source gives a 2-group x 3-period x binary-NCO/NCE observed/latent law computing bridge rank, scalar calibration residual, DiD, ATT, and non-nesting branches from raw cells.

{"status":"completed","mode":"kernel-replace","version":2,"message":"Replaced the singular bridge interval with a finite regime signature Sigma(P) separating PT-only, bridge-only, both, and neither regimes.","proposal_path":"CausalSmith/doc/formalization/research/eid_proximal_did_bridge_frontier/eid_proximal_did_bridge_frontier_v2_proposal.tex","soundness_self_review":{"SC1_hypotheses_in_assumptions":"PASS - every cited assumption in Theorems 1-2 and Conjecture 1 appears in Section 7.","SC2_objects_defined":"PASS - Sigma(P), tau_PT, rho_br, kappa_br, Delta_DiD, Delta_br, B_P, r_P, c_P, and theta_ATT are defined in Section 6.","SC3_corollaries_reduce":"PASS - point-bridge and parallel-trends corners reduce to B_P inverse correction and ordinary DiD respectively.","SC4_intro_kernel_matches_§8":"PASS - Section 4 P4 lists Theorem 1, Conjecture 1, and Theorem 2 with statements matching Section 8.","SC5_consumer_named":"PASS - consumers are ZhangZhangWangEtAl2025, negative-control DiD design audits, and finite bridge diagnostics.","SC6_comparator_promise":"PASS - MiaoGengTchetgen2018, MiaoShiLiTchetgen2024, ZhangZhangWangEtAl2025, SoferRichardsonColicinoSchwartzTchetgen2016, RichardsonYeTchetgen2023, TchetgenParkRichardson2024, and RambachanRoth2023 are matched by Section 8 regime-signature or non-nesting claims.","SC7_no_tautological_iff":"PASS - no flagship iff is defined as the first or smallest point where the target property holds.","SC8_tuning_parameter_bounded":"PASS - no free epsilon or threshold parameter is used; bridge rank and residual are computed from B_P and r_P.","SC9_concrete_witness_exists":"PASS - Exhibit 9.1 gives a rational 2-group x 3-period binary-proxy bridge-only witness.","SC10_focal_object_exhibited":"Focal object: Sigma(P)
Defining equation: Sigma(P) = (tau_PT(P), rho_br(P), kappa_br(P), Delta_DiD(P), Delta_br(P)); rho_br(P)=inf_h ||B_P h-r_P||_infty; kappa_br(P)=|det(B_P)|; Delta_br(P)=Delta_DiD(P)-c_P^T B_P^{-1}r_P when rho_br=0 and kappa_br>0.
RHS symbol provenance: P(W=0,Z=0|G=1)=3/8 RAW; P(W=0,Z=1|G=1)=1/8 RAW; P(W=1,Z=0|G=1)=1/8 RAW; P(W=1,Z=1|G=1)=3/8 RAW; B_P derived row-normalization; r_P=(1/4,0) from observed preperiod means; c_P=(3/4,1/4) RAW post proxy cells; Delta_DiD=1/5 observed outcome-change contrast; tau_PT=1/4 finite latent completion.
Substituted equation: [[3/4,1/4],[1/4,3/4]][h0,h1]^T=[1/4,0]^T.
Computed value: det(B_P)=1/2; h=(3/8,-1/8); Delta_br=1/5-9/32+1/32=-1/20; Sigma(P)=(1/4,0,1/2,1/5,-1/20).
Invariant check 1: proxy probabilities sum to one, LHS=1 RHS=1 result=OK.
Invariant check 2: B_P row sums are 1 and 1, RHS required row sum=1 result=OK.
Invariant check 3: bridge solution bounded and unique, h=(3/8,-1/8), bounds=[-1,1], det=1/2>0 result=OK.
Invariant check 4: DiD and bridge differ with nonzero PT residual, Delta_DiD=1/5, Delta_br=-1/20, tau_PT=1/4 result=OK.
Assumption-substitution screen: tau_PT is displayed by latent completion, not assumed; bounded bridge range is not load-bearing because h lies strictly inside bounds.
SC10 verdict: PASS","SC11_proof_route_non_trivial":"Headline: Conjecture 1 finite non-nesting of DiD and proximal bridge DiD.
Step 0a - unfold Section 6 definitions: Sigma(P), tau_PT, rho_br, kappa_br, Delta_br, and B_P h=r_P; not counted.
Step 0b - apply Section 7 assumptions: consistency, exclusions, finite positivity, bounded bridge class; not counted.
Step 1 (substantive): construct a four-parameter rational latent-type family controlling tau_PT, r_P, B_P, and c_P independently to first order.
Step 2 (substantive): prove the Jacobian to (tau_PT,rho_br,kappa_br,Delta_DiD-Delta_br) has full row rank at Exhibit 9.1.
Step 3 (substantive): apply the implicit-function theorem on the positive-cell simplex to obtain local laws in all four regimes.
Step 4 (substantive): verify the bridge-only branch attains theta_ATT=Delta_br by substituting the unique bridge into the negative-control moment restriction.
Classical result: local transversality of smooth maps on finite probability simplices.
k = 4; PASS iff k >= 3.

banked_reason: Five angles attempted on the proximal-DiD bridge frontier topic; tier ceiling never broke field across 11 reviewer verdicts. Per-angle decomposition: 4 of 5 angles killed by C-sanity arithmetic errors (proposer cannot reliably solve elementary minimax/linear-system problems on its own exhibit); separately, all 5 angles selected pairwise non-equivalence kernels which are structurally field-tier (no impossibility / sharp-efficiency / universal-characterization / dispute-resolution angle attempted). Topic kernel space is shallow at flagship tier; proposer kernel-shape rubric is the bigger lever for future runs.
